query: how do I install the widget service

results.text:
# Widget Guide
The widget service converts raw telemetry into daily reports.
## Install
Install the widget service with `pip install widget-service`.
The installer needs Python 3.11 or newer and about 50 MB of disk space.
### Verify
Run `widget --version` to verify the install succeeded.
## Configure
Configuration lives in `widget.toml` next to the binary.
| key  | meaning          |
|------|------------------|
| rate | polls per minute |
| mode | live or replay   |
## Deploy
The deploy uses a blue-green rollout with health checks before cutover.
Rollbacks complete within two minutes when a health check fails.
```bash
widget deploy --env prod
```

TODO list for the widget service:
- tune the rate key for the busy tenants
- document the replay mode caveats
- retire the legacy exporter once telemetry parity is confirmed

heading_path: ADR-001: Use SQLite for local storage
results.text:
# ADR-001: Use SQLite for local storage
We chose SQLite because the service is single-node and embedded storage
removes an operational dependency. Postgres was rejected for the first
version because it requires a separate server process to operate.

This document has no headings at all, only paragraphs of plain prose.
It exists to exercise the whole-file fallback path of the chunker.
Telemetry batches are flushed every thirty seconds regardless of size.
A second paragraph keeps the fallback honest about paragraph boundaries.

Image file `assets/logo.png`.
- format: png
- dimensions: 3×2
- size: 71 bytes
*(metadata stub — inferred tier; OCR/description is the advanced tier)*
